title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: vignette walks brief Rasch analysis nine-item Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ-9; Kroenke et al. (2001)) using easyRasch2 package. example follows four psychometric criteria proposed Christensen et al. (2021) validation patient-reported outcome measures (PROMs): Unidimensionality — items measure single latent construct. Local independence — conditioning latent trait, item responses independent . Ordered response category thresholds (monotonicity) — moving latent trait increases probability higher categories. Invariance / DIF — item parameters across relevant external groups (e.g. gender). extensive treatment Rasch analysis R, see https://pgmj.github.io/raschrvignette/RaschRvign.html. Bayesian sibling package, see https://pgmj.github.io/easyRaschBayes/ NOTE: simulation-based functions use low number iterations make vignette render faster. use iterations actual analysis work. methods, 500-1000 useful, except conditional infit, 100-400 optimal, depending sample size (Johansson 2025).

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: bundled phq9 dataset 600-respondent random subsample PHQ-9 module U.S. National Health Nutrition Examination Survey (NHANES, September 2024 release) complete responses nine items. NHANES microdata released public domain U.S. federal government.
code: library(easyRasch2) data(phq9) items <- phq9[, paste0("q", 1:9)]   # 9 item columns, scored 0..3 gender <- phq9$gender               # external grouping variables age <- phq9$age                     #  # add item information item_desc <- c(   "Little interest or pleasure in doing things",   "Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless",   "Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much",   "Feeling tired or having little energy", "Poor appetite or overeating",   "Feeling bad about yourself - or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down",   "Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television",   "Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed?",   "Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way" )  item_resp <- c("Not at all","Several days","More than \nhalf the days","Nearly every day") str(items) #> 'data.frame':    600 obs. of  9 variables: #>  $ q1: int  3 0 1 2 3 3 1 3 2 1 ... #>  $ q2: int  3 0 2 3 3 3 1 3 2 0 ... #>  $ q3: int  3 1 3 0 3 1 0 3 2 0 ... #>  $ q4: int  3 1 3 2 3 3 1 3 2 0 ... #>  $ q5: int  3 0 3 2 3 2 0 1 2 0 ... #>  $ q6: int  3 2 3 2 3 2 2 3 3 0 ... #>  $ q7: int  3 3 3 2 3 2 2 3 3 0 ... #>  $ q8: int  1 0 2 0 3 3 0 0 1 0 ... #>  $ q9: int  3 0 0 2 3 1 2 0 0 0 ... summary(rowSums(items)) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>    0.00   10.00   16.00   15.41   21.00   27.00 table(gender, useNA = "ifany") #> gender #> Female   Male   <NA>  #>    426    143     31 summary(age) #>    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max.  #>   15.00   26.00   33.00   36.06   44.00   85.00

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: easyRasch2 provides several complementary unidimensionality diagnostics can combined robust conclusion: item-level conditional infit MSQ statistics (Müller 2020) item-level item-restscore associations Goodman-Kruskal’s gamma (Kreiner 2011) confirmatory factor analysis (CFA) WLSMV estimator ordinal data principal components analysis (PCA) standardised residuals (Chou Wang 2010) Martin-Löf test Monte-Carlo p-values (Christensen Kreiner 2007)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Conditional item infit mean-square statistics flag items whose response patterns deviate Rasch expectation. RMitemInfitCutoff(), per-item highest-density intervals serve reference instead rule--thumb cutoffs (Johansson 2025). MSQ values based conditional estimation (n = 600 complete cases). Cutoff values based 100 simulation iterations (99.9% HDCI). Flagged: overfit = infit range (predictable); underfit = range (noisier). can also get plot summarizing simulated observed item infit, using RMitemInfitCutoffPlot(). Since conditional infit needs complete data, sibling function uses multiple imputation infit useful partial missingness data - RMitemInfitMI() RMitemInfitCutoffMI(). important note RIitemfit() function uses conditional infit, robust different sample sizes makes ZSTD unnecessary (Müller 2020). Müller also questions usefulness outfit, simulation study (Johansson 2025) reached conclusion. Thus, outfit reported unless requested. low item fit value, often referred item “overfit” Rasch model, indicates responses may predictable. often case items general/broad scope relation latent variable, instance asking feeling depressed depression questionnaire. often find overfitting items also residual correlations (local dependencies) items. Overfit may likened much stronger factor loading items confirmatory factor analysis higher level discrimination Item Response Theory model two parameters. high item fit value, often referred “underfit” Rasch model, can indicate several things. Often underfit due multidimensionality question difficult interpret thus noisy response data. latter instance caused question asks two things time, ambiguous reasons. Next visual presentation conditional item fit across latent continuum, respondents split groups based total score. Figure 5. Conditional ICCs five class intervals
code: infit_cut <- RMitemInfitCutoff(items, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                            seed = 3) RMitemInfit(items, cutoff = infit_cut) RMitemICCPlot(items, class_intervals = 5)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Item-restscore uses Goodman-Kruskal’s gamma shows expected observed correlation item score based rest items (Kreiner 2011). Similarly, inverted, item infit, lower observed correlation value expected indicates underfit, item may belong dimension. higher expected observed value indicates overfitting possibly redundant item. Overfitting items often also show issues local dependency. Compared infit, item-restscore often flags overfit items (based experience), less often flags underfit items (based simulation study (Johansson 2025)). Item-restscore associations (n = 600 complete cases). Flagged (adj. p < .05): overfit = observed expected (-discrimination, often local dependence); underfit = (-discrimination, often multidimensionality/noise).
code: RMitemRestscore(items)

what: CFA-based cutoffs for CFI / RMSEA and item loadings
title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: RMdimCFACutoff() simulates data unidimensional PCM fits unidimensional ordinal CFA simulated dataset, building parametric-bootstrap reference distribution model fit indices (SRMR, CFI, RMSEA) per-item standardized factor loadings. returns simulation object; RMdimCFA() fits CFA observed data tabulates observed fit indices loadings simulated reference. Values extreme simulated cut-offs extreme plausible unidimensional data generating process. Partial Credit Model posterior-predictive CFA fit-index check. Observed CFA fit (one-factor, lavaan WLSMV, ordered = TRUE; n = 600) vs simulated null PCM unidimensionality (100 iterations n = 600). Cutoffs one-sided 99th percentile; flagged observed value lies worst 1% null unfavourable direction. Standardized factor loadings (one-factor, lavaan WLSMV; n = 600) vs simulated expected range PCM unidimensionality (100 iterations n = 600). Expected range two-sided central 99% interval simulated loadings; Flagged = / range. RMdimCFAPlot(cfa_cut, data = items) returns list two figures: $loadings (observed loadings simulated expected ranges) $fit (simulated fit-index distributions observed values overlaid).
code: cfa_cut <- RMdimCFACutoff(items, iterations = 100, parallel = FALSE,                        seed = 2) cfa_tabs <- RMdimCFA(items, cutoff = cfa_cut) cfa_tabs$fit cfa_tabs$loadings

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: fitting Rasch model, residuals contain systematic structure. largest eigenvalue residual correlation matrix can considered headline diagnostic; value clearly simulation-based cut-suggest secondary dimension. However, eigenvalue largest value support unidimensionality. Partial Credit Model (600 complete cases, 9 items). Total observed variance: 51.3% explained measures, 48.7% unexplained (basis PCA; n = 600 non-extreme cases). First-contrast cutoff = 1.217 based 100 simulation iterations (99th percentile). Also interest plot item standardised loadings first residual contrast item locations. figure can helpful identify clusters data, perhaps related local dependency /multidimensionality. Figure 6. Standardised loadings first residual contrast

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Local independence (LD) can assessed multiple methods. Yen’s Q_3 statistic (Yen 1984) correlation person-item standardised residuals every item pair. Pair-wise Q_3 values simulation-based cut-flag LD (Christensen et al. 2017). Dynamic cut-: 0.031 (mean Q3 -0.109 + 0.14). Global simulation cutoff (99th pctl max-mean Q3) 100 iterations. Correlations exceeding cut-may indicate local dependence; see per-pair table detail. powerful Q_3 test, one can use simulated cutoffs object plot expected range residual correlations item-pair compare observed value. ’ll limit output 6 item-pairs deviate . Positive gamma indicates positive local dependence items. Showing top 6 36 pairs |gamma|. Direction 1: rest score = total - Item2. Partial gamma LD analysis (n = 600 complete cases). title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: use two complementary DIF assessments. Andersen likelihood-ratio test (LRT, Andersen 1973) partitions sample external variable, refits model subgroup, compares item locations. partial gamma approach (Kreiner 2007; Christensen et al. 2021) looks association item responses external variable conditional rest-score. run gender variable (dropping respondents missing gender):
code: keep    <- !is.na(gender) items_g <- items[keep, ] gender_g <- droplevels(gender[keep])

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Figure 10. Andersen LR-test DIF locations gender plot shows item threshold locations estimated gender group corresponding confidence band.
code: RMdifLR(items_g, dif_var = gender_g, level = "threshold")

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: Partial gamma DIF analysis (n = 569 complete cases). Positive gamma indicates higher scores higher DIF group levels. model-based DIF analysis can handle continuous covariates interactions (e.g. age × gender), see ?RMdifTree (Strobl et al. 2015; Henninger et al. 2025).
code: RMdifGamma(items_g, dif_var = gender_g)

title: A Rasch analysis workflow with easyRasch2
text: RMreliability() reports four reliability metrics: person separation reliability (PSI); Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) estimate derived posterior person-location uncertainty using plausible values; Cronbach’s alpha; Empirical reliability (using mirt::empirical_rxx(). PSI, alpha empirical can use bootstrap confidence intervals. reliability metrics range 0 1, higher values indicating better separation/precision. Reliability 9 items, n = 600. PSI WLE-based separation reliability excludes min/max scoring respondents.
code: RMreliability(items, draws = 200, rmu_iter = 20, parallel = FALSE,               seed = 5)

title: Psychometric Analysis with Rasch Measurement Theory
text: Estimation: Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) via eRm, psychotools, iarm item parameters; Weighted Likelihood Estimation (WLE) person parameters. now extends Yen’s Q3 residual correlations (RMlocdepQ3(), CML/WLE default); mirt (MML) retained optional engine (estimator = "MML") places without CML alternative (e.g., reliability indices). code: library(easyRasch2) data("pcmdat2", package = "eRm") options(mc.cores = 4) set.seed(42)  # Conditional item infit with simulation-based cutoffs simfit <- RMitemInfitCutoff(pcmdat2, iterations = 250) RMitemInfit(pcmdat2, cutoff = simfit)  # Test of unidimensionality via posterior-predictive ordinal CFA cfa_sim <- RMdimCFACutoff(pcmdat2, iterations = 250)   # simulated reference tabs <- RMdimCFA(pcmdat2, cutoff = cfa_sim)            # observed vs expected tabs$fit                                                # fit-index table tabs$loadings                                           # per-item loading table plots <- RMdimCFAPlot(cfa_sim, data = pcmdat2)          # list of 2 ggplots plots$loadings                                          # observed vs expected loadings plots$fit                                               # fit-index distributions  # DIF analysis via Andersen's LR test grp <- factor(sample(c("A", "B"), nrow(pcmdat2), replace = TRUE)) RMdifLR(pcmdat2, dif_var = grp)  # Rasch-tree DIF with effect-size classification on continuous + # categorical covariates simultaneously covs <- data.frame(   group = grp,   band  = sample(c("low", "high"), nrow(pcmdat2), replace = TRUE) ) RMdifTree(pcmdat2, covariates = covs)

title: Partial Gamma DIF Analysis — RMdifGamma
text: Partial gamma (Bjorner et al., 1998) measures association item response exogenous grouping variable, controlling total score. Values near 0 indicate DIF. Recommended interpretive thresholds (Bjorner et al., 1998): negligible DIF: gamma within \([-0.21, 0.21]\), gamma significantly different 0. Slight moderate DIF: gamma within \([-0.31, 0.31]\) (outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\)), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). Moderate large DIF: gamma outside \([-0.31, 0.31]\), significantly outside \([-0.21, 0.21]\). iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: Splits Rasch model external grouping variable using eRm::LRtest() reports per-group item locations (per-group threshold locations) together standard errors. single function replaces four legacy helpers (RIdifTableLR, RIdifThreshTblLR, RIdifFigureLR, RIdifThreshFigLR) exposing two underlying axes – level (item threshold) output (data.frame, kable, ggplot) – arguments. data preparation pipeline feeds six combinations.

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
code: RMdifLR(   data,   dif_var,   model = c("auto", "PCM", "RM"),   level = c("item", "threshold"),   output = c("ggplot", "kable", "dataframe"),   cutoff = 0.5,   conf = 0.95,   sort = FALSE )

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: data data.frame matrix item responses (non-negative integers, 0-based). One column per item, one row per person. Person IDs grouping variables must included – pass grouping variable separately via dif_var. dif_var Vector length nrow(data) (factor, character, numeric) defining DIF grouping variable. Coerced factor; unused levels dropped. Rows dif_var NA dropped message. Must result least 2 groups cleaning. model One "auto" (default), "PCM", "RM". "auto" fits eRm::RM() data dichotomous (max response = 1) eRm::PCM() otherwise. "RM" errors polytomous data. level One "item" (default) "threshold". "item" reports item's mean threshold location per group; "threshold" reports individual threshold per group. dichotomous (RM) data two views equivalent (one threshold per item). output One "ggplot" (default), "kable", "dataframe". data.frame view always carries Flagged logical column; kable view bolds flagged rows; ggplot view shows confidence intervals. cutoff Numeric NULL. Threshold (logits) Flagged column: row flagged MaxDiff > cutoff, MaxDiff difference largest smallest per-group location item (threshold). Set NULL suppress flagging. Default 0.5. conf Numeric (0, 1). Confidence level used ggplot error bars. Default 0.95. sort Logical. kable output : sort rows MaxDiff (descending). Default FALSE.

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: data.frame, knitr_kable object, ggplot object, depending output. data.frame one row per item (level = "item") per item x threshold (level = "threshold"), columns Item (Threshold threshold level), one numeric column per group level, column unsplit fit, MaxDiff, Flagged (cutoff non-NULL), matching SE_* columns. Andersen LR test result attached attr(result, "lr_test") data.frame, kable footnote, ggplot caption (LR \(\chi^2\), df, p-value).

title: DIF analysis via Andersen's likelihood-ratio test — RMdifLR
text: Partial Credit Model (PCM) fitted default polytomous data dichotomous Rasch Model (RM) fitted responses 0/1; can overridden via model. data.frame kable outputs, locations reported centred eRm parameterisation returned eRm::thresholds(). Per-group fits come eRm::LRtest(..., splitcr = dif_var); unsplit fit (column) model fitted full dataset. Andersen LR statistic, df, p-value reported lr_test attribute / caption come directly LRtest()'s return value. cell_spec()-style HTML cell colouring used legacy easyRasch package dropped favour logical Flagged column (bold rendering kable output), kable renders correctly HTML, LaTeX, pipe/markdown.

what: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
text: Detects Differential Item Functioning (DIF) recursively splitting sample one covariates using psychotree::raschtree() (dichotomous data) psychotree::pctree() (polytomous data), computes per-split effect-size measures every item – Mantel-Haenszel odds-ratio (ETS Delta scale) dichotomous data, partial gamma coefficient polytomous data – classifies ETS /B/C categories. Optionally, iterative purification step (Holland & Thayer, 1988; Bjorner et al., 1998) applied, tree stability across resamples can assessed via stablelearner::stabletree().

title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
code: RMdifTree(   data,   covariates,   model = c("auto", "PCM", "RM"),   effect_size = c("auto", "MH", "pgamma"),   purification = c("none", "iterative"),   p_adj = c("none", "fdr", "bonferroni"),   thresholds = c(0.21, 0.31),   alpha = 0.05,   prune_negligible = FALSE,   stability = FALSE,   stability_B = 100L,   stability_sampler = c("subsampling", "bootstrap"),   min_n_per_level = 20L,   on_rescale = c("message", "warning", "stop"),   output = c("kable", "dataframe", "tree", "plot"),   ... )

title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
text: Continuous covariates (e.g., age years) interactions among multiple covariates handled natively model-based recursive-partitioning machinery partykit / psychotree. ETS Delta-scale Mantel-Haenszel. MH common odds ratio \(\hat{\alpha}_{MH}\) mapped ETS Delta scale via \(\Delta_{MH} = -2.35 \log \hat{\alpha}_{MH}\). classification rules (Holland & Thayer, 1988; Zwick, 2012) : Class : \(|\Delta_{MH}| < 1\) test \(\Delta_{MH} = 0\) rejected alpha. Class C: \(|\Delta_{MH}| \ge 1.5\) test \(|\Delta_{MH}| \le 1\) rejected alpha. Class B: otherwise. easyRasch2 uses sign convention effecttree: positive \(\Delta\) indicates item difficult second (reference) group. Partial gamma. iarm::partgam_DIF() provides gamma estimate SE per item. ETS-style classification follows Bjorner et al. (1998): \(|\gamma| < 0.21\) test \(\gamma = 0\) rejected alpha; C \(|\gamma| > 0.31\) test \(|\gamma| \le 0.21\) rejected alpha; B otherwise. Continuous interaction effects. recursive partitioning step automatically handles continuous covariates (parameter-instability test searches optimal cutpoint) multivariate interactions (later splits conditional earlier ones). assess sensitivity variable selection cutpoints particular sample, set stability = TRUE. Stability assessment. stability = TRUE, tree-fitting call replayed stability_B resamples data. returned stabletree object reports, per covariate, proportion resamples selected split, (continuous covariates) empirical distribution cutpoints. Stability independent effect-size classification – stable split negligible effect still negligible, large effect unstable split interpreted care. Cost roughly stability_B times original fitting time. Acknowledgement. effect-size machinery – MH partial gamma per node, iterative purification, ETS /B/C classification – follows implementations Henninger & Radek GitHub packages raschtreeMH effecttree. relevant code adapted MIT licence (see file header).

title: Tree-based DIF analysis with effect-size classification — RMdifTree
code: # \donttest{ if (requireNamespace("psychotree", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("partykit", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("difR", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("iarm", quietly = TRUE)) {   data("DIFSimPC", package = "psychotree")    items <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(DIFSimPC$resp))   covs  <- DIFSimPC[, c("age", "gender", "motivation")]    # Default: kable of per-split effect sizes   RMdifTree(items, covariates = covs)    # Tidy data.frame -- one row per (split node x item)   df <- RMdifTree(items, covariates = covs, output = "dataframe")   df[df$Flagged, ]    # Tree object (for plotting via partykit::plot)   tree <- RMdifTree(items, covariates = covs, output = "tree")   plot(tree)    # Stability assessment refits the tree on B resamples.   # (use more resamples, e.g. 100+, in real analyses)   if (requireNamespace("stablelearner", quietly = TRUE)) {     kbl <- RMdifTree(items, covariates = covs,                      purification = "iterative", p_adj = "fdr",                      stability = TRUE, stability_B = 25)     kbl                          # main effect-size kable     attr(kbl, "stability_kable") # pre-rendered stability kable     attr(kbl, "stability")       # raw stability data.frame   } }  #> Error in root.matrix(switch(vcov, opg = chol2inv(chol(meat)), info = bread,  :  #>   Matrix is not positive semidefinite #> Error in root.matrix(switch(vcov, opg = chol2inv(chol(meat)), info = bread,  :  #>   Matrix is not positive semidefinite #> Stability assessment notes (suppressed during fitting): 137 'minimum score not zero' rescaling warning(s); 21 'items with null categories' warning(s). These reflect sparsity in some resamples and are normal sampling variability; selection-frequency and cutpoint summaries are based on resamples that completed successfully. #>     Variable    Type Selected_orig Selection_freq_pct Cutpoint_mean Cutpoint_sd #> 1        age numeric          TRUE                100       42.9706      8.1556 #> 2     gender  factor          TRUE                100            NA          NA #> 3 motivation ordered         FALSE                  4            NA          NA # }

title: Martin-Löf Test of Unidimensionality — RMdimMartinLof
text: Likelihood-ratio test unidimensionality priori specified multidimensional alternative, generalised polytomous Rasch / partial credit models (Christensen, Bjorner, Kreiner, & Petersen, 2002). p-value obtained parametric-bootstrap (Monte Carlo) sampling unidimensional null, following Christensen & Kreiner (2007), asymptotic chi-square approximation biased toward conservatism realistic sample sizes – especially polytomous items, degrees freedom can large.

title: Martin-Löf Test of Unidimensionality — RMdimMartinLof
text: routine screening tool. test requires priori partition items subscales; using post-hoc , e.g., partition suggested RMdimResidualPCA()'s PC1 sign inflate Type-error rate. source papers state explicitly. Test statistic. items partitioned D subscales, total score \(t\) subscores \((t_1, \ldots, t_D)\) (Christensen et al. 2002, eq. 22): $$T = 2\Bigl[\sum_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}   n_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}\log(n_{t_1, \ldots, t_D}/N)   - \sum_t n_t\log(n_t/N)   - \ell_C(\hat{\epsilon}) + \sum_d \ell_C(\hat{\epsilon}^{(d)})\Bigr]$$ \(\ell_C\) conditional log-likelihood \(\hat{\epsilon}^{(d)}\) CML estimates d-th subscale alone. CML fits use psychotools::raschmodel() (RM) psychotools::pcmodel() (PCM) speed. Monte Carlo sampling H0. Following Christensen & Kreiner (2007): () sample N total scores empirical score distribution \(n_t/N\); (b) sampled score, sample item-response vector conditional distribution \(p(x \mid t, \hat{\epsilon})\) given eq. 4 paper. dichotomous data fast algorithm Christensen & Kreiner (2007, p. 23) used (sample without replacement weighted item easinesses). polytomous data recursive \(\gamma\)-function approach used, item's response sampled conditional remaining items' joint score distribution (computed via psychotools::elementary_symmetric_functions()). Iterations fail (e.g., simulated dataset empty category item) silently dropped. Item parameters estimated observed data held fixed across MC iterations. Christensen & Kreiner (2007) use extended likelihood function (Tjur, 1982) empirical score distribution non-parametric estimate latent distribution, distributional assumption \(\theta\) needed.

title: Standardised Residuals from the Joint Subscore Distribution — RMdimMartinLofResiduals
text: cell joint subscore table (indexed \((t_1, \ldots, t_D)\)), conditional probability H0 given total score \(t = \sum_d t_d\) $$p(t_1, \ldots, t_D \mid t) = \prod_d \gamma^{(d)}_{t_d} / \gamma_t,$$ expected count \(e = n_t \cdot p\), residual \((o - e) / \sqrt{n_t \cdot p \cdot (1 - p)}\). CML estimates unidimensional model used \(\gamma\)-functions. Reading table (D = 2): unidimensional null, residuals patternless roughly N(0, 1). Multidimensionality positively correlated dimensions typically shows positive residuals corners antidiagonal (high t1 + low t2, low t1 + high t2) negative residuals near antidiagonal centre (matched subscores). Negatively correlated dimensions show positive residuals table corners (high/low low/high) negative residuals high/high low/low. See Christensen et al. (2002, section7) worked example. Cells total score observed cases (n_t = 0) uninformative dropped output.

text: polytomous item response categories \(0, 1, \ldots, K_i\) threshold parameters \(\delta_{,1}, \ldots, \delta_{,K_i}\) (CML, psychotools), PCM category probability $$P(X_i = k \mid \theta) = \frac{\exp(\sum_{j=1}^{k}   (\theta - \delta_{,j}))}{\sum_{k'=0}^{K_i} \exp(\sum_{j=1}^{k'}   (\theta - \delta_{,j}))},$$ empty sum (\(k = 0\)) taken zero. dichotomous items function fits Rasch model treats item difficulty \(\delta_i = -\beta_i\) single threshold, recovering standard two-category logistic ICC. colour mapping uses scale_color_viridis_c() integer category value, natural ordering response categories preserved visually — low categories one end palette, high categories . category_labels provided, legend uses labels (e.g., "Never" / "Sometimes" / "Often") colour mapping stays integer category value. title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMitemInfit
text: Infit MSQ weighted fit statistic emphasises deviations near item location. Values close 1.0 indicate good fit. Values substantially 1.0 suggest underfit (unexpected responses), values substantially 1.0 suggest overfit (overly predictable responses). definition "substantially" depends several factors sample size, needs determined simulation using RMitemInfitCutoff. general rule--thumb value correct. Conditional infit MSQ statistics computed via iarm::out_infit(), uses conditional distribution sufficient statistics (Müller, 2020). complete cases (rows without NA) used conditional fit calculation. Item parameters estimated conditional maximum likelihood via psychotools::pcmodel() (dichotomous item 2-category PCM); conditional infit/outfit MSQ comes iarm::out_infit() invariant estimation engine. Per-item average locations means CML thresholds, person-location reference mean Warm WLE estimates. Relative item location defined item's average location minus sample mean person location, providing measure item targeting. iarm package must installed (Suggests, Imports). Bootstrap p-values. p_value = TRUE, item's observed infit compared simulated null distribution (cutoff$results). per-item statistic residual studentised bootstrap mean SD – deliberately empirical SD rather Wilson-Hilferty / ZSTD transform, uninformative conditional MSQ (Müller, 2020). marginal p-value two-sided Monte-Carlo p-value (1 + #{|t*| >= |t|}) / (B + 1). correction = "fwer" family-wise adjustment uses Westfall-Young studentised-max step-, exploits bootstrap dependence among items powerful Bonferroni/Holm (Ferreira, 2024); validity rests subset pivotality. "fdr_bh"/"fdr_by" apply Benjamini-Hochberg / Benjamini- Yekutieli instead. model-conditional, sample-size-sensitive p-values reported alongside simulated effect-size band, place . p-values can smaller 1 / (B + 1), studentised-max (FWER) correction liberal simulation small (bootstrap mean/SD used studentisation noisy). least 1000 iterations RMitemInfitCutoff() recommended – simulations family-wise error rate controlled nominal level – warning issued simulation smaller. marginal p-values well calibrated even hundred iterations.

title: Conditional Item Infit MSQ — RMitemInfit
text: marginal p-value controls error rate single comparison: one item (item pair) decided advance relevant value. scanning k comparisons flagging whichever fall alpha tests k hypotheses , chance least one false flag inflates roughly \(1 - (1 - \alpha)^k\) (e.g. 34% k = 8 alpha = 0.05) – even every marginal p-value correctly calibrated. corrected (adjusted) p-value controls : correction = "fwer" bounds probability false flag (strict, lower power), "fdr_bh" / "fdr_by" bound expected proportion false flags among raised (lenient middle ground). Rule thumb: use marginal p-value single pre-specified comparison, corrected p-value screening whole table – usual workflow.

title: Person Locations for a Rasch / Partial Credit Model — RMpersonParameters
text: Item parameters obtained (CML MML, taken item_params) treated fixed. person's theta estimated items answered. WLE solves Warm's weighted-likelihood score equation l'(theta) + J(theta) / (2 (theta)) = 0 bracketed root finding, bias-correction term J / (2 ) keeps equation solvable boundaries. SEM 1 / sqrt((theta)). Unlike MLE, Warm's estimator therefore yields finite locations minimum maximum possible scores (sensible step beyond next-extreme score rather +/-Inf; cf. Warm, 1989), matching established implementations catR TAM. scores still marked extreme column extrapolated carry large standard errors. root lies outside theta_range boundary returned NA SEM. EAP integrates pattern likelihood normal prior quadrature grid; estimate posterior mean SEM posterior standard deviation. Unlike WLE, EAP finite extreme scores prior shrinks inward, cost depending assumed prior. prior actually used – including marginal-ML estimate prior_sd left NULL – reported kable caption attached result attr(result, "prior").

what: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: Computes three reliability indices Rasch / partial credit model: Person Separation Index (PSI) – WLE-based separation reliability – marginal reliability (native, CML test information integrated estimated normal latent density), Relative Measurement Uncertainty (RMU) via RMUreliability() applied plausible values mirt::fscores().

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
code: RMreliability(   data,   conf_int = 0.95,   draws = 1000,   rmu_iter = 50,   estim = "WLE",   boot = FALSE,   boot_iter = 200,   parallel = TRUE,   n_cores = NULL,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE,   theta_range = c(-10, 10),   output = "kable" )

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). conf_int Numeric (0, 1). HDCI width bootstrap CIs RMU. Default 0.95. draws Integer. Number plausible-value draws drawn mirt model RMU calculation. Default 1000. gives stable RMU; computational cost mostly linear. rmu_iter Integer. Number times RMUreliability() repeated set plausible-value draws (repetition uses fresh random column split). Estimates averaged across repetitions stabilise split-induced variability. Default 50. estim Character. Theta estimator used mirt::fscores() RMU plausible-value seed. One "WLE" (default), "EAP", "MAP", "ML". Plausible draws produced Metropolis-Hastings. (PSI marginal reliability computed natively use .) boot Logical. TRUE, run non-parametric bootstrap obtain CIs PSI Marginal reliability. Default FALSE. boot_iter Integer. Number bootstrap iterations boot = TRUE. Default 200. parallel Logical. Use parallel processing via mirai bootstrap available. Default TRUE. n_cores Integer NULL. Number parallel workers. NULL, getOption("mc.cores") checked first; neither set, bootstrapping falls back sequential. seed Integer NULL. Master random seed reproducibility. verbose Logical. Print progress messages progress bar bootstrap. Default FALSE. theta_range Numeric length-2 vector. Theta limits passed mirt::fscores(). Default c(-10, 10). output Character. "kable" (default) formatted knitr::kable() table, "dataframe" underlying data.frame.

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: output = "kable": knitr_kable object one row per metric. output = "dataframe": data.frame columns metric, estimate, lower, upper, notes.

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability
text: Confidence intervals PSI Marginal reliability obtained non-parametric bootstrap (resampling respondents; three indices recomputed natively per resample, model refitted mirt). RMU interval HDCI correlations across plausible-value draws, averaged rmu_iter random splits draws. Marginal reliability native Green (1984) coefficient, \(1 - \overline{1/(\theta)}/\sigma^2\), test information \((\theta)\) summed CML item parameters average error variance taken estimated normal latent density \(N(0, \sigma^2)\) (\(\sigma\) marginal ML). Unlike mirt::marginal_rxx(), assumes \(N(0,1)\), integrates estimated latent variance, correct Rasch logit scale (\(\sigma\) typically well 1, \(N(0,1)\) assumption underestimates reliability). model-based complement sample-based PSI; large gap two flags -target non-normal sample. PSI WLE-based separation reliability, \(1 - \overline{SEM^2} / \mathrm{Var}(\hat\theta)\), computed CML item thresholds (psychotools) Warm's WLE person locations / analytic SEMs. Respondents extreme (min/max) raw scores excluded – boundary estimates inflate person variance overstate reliability. (Earlier versions used eRm::SepRel() MLE; values can differ, noticeably scales many extreme scorers, e.g. dichotomous items.) RMU Bignardi, Kievit, & Bürkner (2025), modified use mirt plausible values rather fully Bayesian posterior draws (see Mislevy, 1991, plausible-values framework). Bootstrap iterations fail converge silently dropped.

title: Reliability metrics for a Rasch model — RMreliability

D., Humphreys, L. G., Linn, R. L., & Reckase, M. D. (1984). Technical Guidelines Assessing Computerized Adaptive Tests. Journal Educational Measurement, 21(4), 347–360. doi:10.1111/j.1745-3984.1984.tb01039.x Mislevy, R. J. (1991). Randomization-Based Inference Latent Variables Complex Samples. Psychometrika, 56(2), 177-196. doi:10.1007/BF02294457 Adams, R. J. (2005). Reliability measurement design effect. Studies Educational Evaluation, 31(2), 162-172. doi:10.1016/j.stueduc.2005.05.008

PSI is the WLE-based separation reliability and excludes min/max scoring respondents. #>  #> |Metric           | Estimate| Lower (95% HDCI)| Upper (95% HDCI)|Notes                       | #> |:----------------|--------:|----------------:|----------------:|:---------------------------| #> |Cronbach's alpha |    0.754|            0.701|            0.813|25 bootstrap resamples      | #> |PSI              |    0.725|            0.673|            0.768|25 bootstrap resamples      | #> |Marginal         |    0.695|            0.602|            0.769|25 bootstrap resamples      | #> |RMU (WLE)        |    0.753|            0.684|            0.818|1000 PVs, 50 RMU iterations | # }

title: Raw-Score to Logit Score Transformation Table — RMscoreSE
text: function automatically detects whether data dichotomous (max score 1) polytomous (max score > 1) selects appropriate Rasch / Partial Credit model. method = "WLE" fits model CML psychotools::pcmodel(), centres item thresholds grand-mean-zero, solves Warm's weighted-likelihood equation raw score engine used RMpersonParameters(); two functions therefore report identical locations standard errors. Warm's bias correction yields finite locations even minimum maximum scores (root outside theta_range clamped boundary NA SE). reported logit_se information-based standard error 1 / sqrt((theta)), matching catR, TAM Rasch software. method = "EAP" fits model mirt::mirt(..., itemtype = "Rasch") (MML) obtains sum-score-based EAP estimates posterior SDs via mirt::fscores(method = "EAPsum", full.scores = FALSE, full.scores.SE = TRUE). EAP estimates finite score boundaries (prior shrinks inward), depend assumed normal prior theta. Item parameters MML differ slightly CML values used WLE path; well-behaved data difference small.

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: data data.frame matrix item responses. Items must scored starting 0 (non-negative integers). Missing values (NA) allowed. robust Logical. FALSE (default), histogram annotations use mean ± SD. TRUE, median ± MAD used instead. sort_items Character string controlling item ordering y-axis bottom panel. "data" (default) preserves column order data (first item top). "location" sorts items average threshold location (easiest top, hardest bottom). bins Integer. Number bins histograms. Default number unique scores plus one, less 15. xlim Numeric vector length 2. Initial lower upper limits shared x-axis. Automatically expanded person item threshold values fall outside limits. ci_level Numeric. Confidence level item threshold error bars. Default 0.95 (95% CI). Set NULL hide error bars. person_fill Fill colour person histogram. Default "#0072B2" (blue). threshold_fill Fill colour item threshold histogram. Default "#D55E00" (vermillion). height_ratios Numeric vector length 3 specifying relative heights top (person), middle (threshold), bottom (dot-whisker) panels. Default c(3, 2, 5). output Character string. "patchwork" (default) returns combined patchwork plot. "list" returns named list three ggplot objects (p1, p2, p3) customisation.

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting
text: Together, top middle panels form back--back histogram makes easy assess whether test well-targeted sample. Estimation method selection. function checks whether item response category fewer 3 observations. categories least 3 responses, item threshold locations standard errors estimated via Conditional Maximum Likelihood (CML) using psychotools::pcmodel() (dichotomous item 2-category PCM). category fewer 3 responses, function falls back Marginal Maximum Likelihood (MML) estimation via mirt::mirt() itemtype = "Rasch" SE = TRUE, numerically stable sparse-category conditions. message emitted MML fallback used. cases, item threshold locations centered (shifted grand mean thresholds equals zero). Person estimates obtained Warm's weighted likelihood (WLE) fitted item thresholds, consistent rest package. WLE finite extreme scores, -zero perfect responders located rather dropped. Confidence intervals item thresholds based Wald-type intervals: threshold estimate ± z × SE, z standard normal quantile corresponding ci_level. ggplot2 patchwork packages must installed (Suggests, Imports).

title: Person-Item Targeting Plot (Wright Map) — RMtargeting

what: easyRasch2: Psychometric Analysis with Rasch Measurement Theory — easyRasch2-package
title: easyRasch2: Psychometric Analysis with Rasch Measurement Theory — easyRasch2-package
text: Streamlines reproducible Rasch measurement theory analyses ordinal item-response data, combining estimation routines 'eRm', 'psychotools', 'mirt', 'iarm', 'lavaan' consistent diagnostic, plotting, reporting layers. Covers four basic psychometric criteria summarised Christensen et al. (2021) doi:10.1111/sms.13908  – unidimensionality, local independence, ordered response category thresholds, invariance across subgroups – together item fit, targeting, reliability, category functioning, descriptive item-response plots. distinguishing feature use simulation-based critical values replace rule--thumb cutoffs conditional infit mean-square, Yen's Q3 local-dependence statistic, largest residual-PCA eigenvalue, ordinal CFA fit indices. Outputs knitr::kable() tables 'ggplot2' figures suitable direct inclusion 'Quarto' 'R Markdown' reports.

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: large consolidation release preparation next CRAN submission. headline change whole package now runs single estimation engine — conditional maximum likelihood (CML) item parameters via psychotools Warm’s weighted-likelihood (WLE) person locations — replacing previous mix eRm (MLE) mirt (MML/EAP). top : new CFA loading diagnostics, three new functions person+item parameters person fit, bootstrap p-values multiplicity correction, round naming/output consistency fixes. release eRm used RMdifLR() (Andersen’s LR test) mirt RMU plausible values. Many items shift reported numbers slightly: underlying statistics unchanged, estimation engine (WLE locations finite extreme scores, extreme-score cases now retained rather dropped).

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: Observed item statistics — RMitemRestscore(), RMitemInfit(), RMitemCatProb(), RMitemHierarchy(), RMtargeting() — moved CML (psychotools) + WLE. conditional iarm statistics engine-invariant; minor things move common grand-mean-zero scale (Relative_location via WLE person mean; threshold SEs ~%; polytomous ICC curve position; targeting person distribution). RMdifLR() deliberately stays eRm::LRtest(). Parameter / score functions — shared .rasch_fit_cml() behind RMitemParameters(), RMpersonParameters(), RMscoreSE() now fits CML via psychotools. Locations, person estimates score SEs unchanged (match eRm ~5e-5); RMitemParameters()’s threshold SEs shift slightly (psychotools vcov vs eRm delta method, ~%). RMscoreSE() — WLE method now reports information-based SE 1/sqrt((theta)) (matching RMpersonParameters(), catR, TAM); point estimates unchanged, SEs differ (larger) score extremes, WLE solver/centring now shared RMpersonParameters(). RMreliability() — empirical reliability replaced native marginal reliability (Green 1984; “Empirical” row now “Marginal”), mirt::marginal_rxx() uses N(0,1), integrates estimated latent variance, correct Rasch logit scale; PSI now WLE-based separation reliability (1 - mean(SEM^2)/Var(theta), excluding extreme scorers), replacing eRm::SepRel() + mirt. bootstrap now fully native (mirt per resample). PSI can differ noticeably scales many extreme scorers; alpha RMU unchanged. Simulation-based tools now generate refit CML/WLE engine: RMlocdepQ3()/RMlocdepQ3Cutoff(), RMitemInfitCutoff(), RMitemInfitMI()/RMitemInfitCutoffMI(), RMitemRestscoreBoot(), RMdimResidualPCA()/RMdimResidualPCACutoff(), RMdimCFACutoff(), RMdimMartinLof(), RMdifGammaCutoff(), RMlocdepGammaCutoff(). eRm-based internal extract_item_thresholds() helper removed. Conditional / scale-invariant statistics (infit/outfit, item-restscore classification, Martin-Löf Monte-Carlo p-value, pooled MI infit MSQ/SE) unchanged; residual-based simulated quantities shift slightly — Q3 values/cut-offs/flags (statistic unchanged; observed-vs-old-MML correlation typically >0.95), PCA eigenvalues / variance partition / cut-, gamma cut-offs. RMlocdepQ3()/RMlocdepQ3Cutoff() keep previous engine available via estimator = "MML" (default "CML"); estimator stored cutoff object reused RMlocdepQ3()/RMlocdepQ3Plot() (mismatch overridden warning). RMitemInfitMI()’s pooled-SE caveat (Müller 2020) now documented.

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: RMpersonFit() — per-respondent conditional infit/outfit MSQ standardized log-likelihood lz, computed per response pattern (handles partial missingness, biased person estimate residual). Significance Monte-Carlo resampling fitted model, unreliable asymptotic null (Sinharay 2016; Müller 2020). Output table, data.frame, named list person-fit maps; zstd = TRUE adds Wilson-Hilferty transform (comparability , inference); flag = "underfit" restricts flagging validity-relevant direction. RMitemParameters() — item difficulty (dichotomous) Andrich-threshold (polytomous) parameters long/wide format, optional SEs Wald CIs. RMpersonParameters() — per-respondent theta SEM computed response pattern (handles partial missingness), via Warm’s WLE (default) EAP normal prior (SD estimated marginal ML unless fixed).

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: CFA now also reports per-item standardized factor loadings (observed vs simulated expected range), restructured match simulation tools: RMdimCFACutoff() now simulate-— returns simulation object (null distributions + cutoffs fit indices loadings); longer computes observed fit, output = "kable" errors pointer RMdimCFA(). (Breaking.) RMdimCFA() (new) takes data + required cutoff object returns list two tables, $fit (CFI/RMSEA/SRMR) $loadings (observed loading vs two-sided expected range), kables (default) data.frames. RMdimCFAPlot(cutoff_res, data) now returns list two ggplots ($loadings, RMitemInfitCutoffPlot() style; $fit, faceted fit-index distributions) requires data. (Breaking: previously single ggplot.)

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: RMitemInfit() gains p_value: per-item bootstrap p-values simulated null (needs full RMitemInfitCutoff() object), studentised bootstrap SD, Westfall-Young studentised-max FWER (default) Benjamini-Hochberg / Benjamini-Yekutieli FDR. ≥1000 cutoff iterations recommended (warning otherwise); default (p_value = FALSE) unchanged. (Ferreira 2024; Westfall & Young 1993.) RMlocdepQ3() full cutoff object now returns $matrix plus per-pair $pairs table (observed Q3 vs simulated low/high band, directional flag, sorted departure per-pair median, optional n_pairs cap). optional p_value layer folds one-sided bootstrap p-values excess local dependence (FWER/FDR correction) $pairs. RMlocdepQ3() RMitemInfit() share “Multiple comparisons” help section.

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: Naming aligned across functions (deprecation aliases): RMdimResidualPCA() plot output now output = "ggplot" ("loadings" kept backward-compatible alias); RMtargeting() output = "figure" → "patchwork" (matching RMitemICCPlot()); RMitemRestscore() p.adj → p_adj; RMitemInfitCutoffPlot() output → statistic. Flagged column now labels misfit direction ("overfit" / "underfit" / "") instead logical TRUE/FALSE RMitemInfit() / RMitemInfitMI() (column type changes logical character). RMitemRestscore() gains Flagged column drops redundant significance-star Location columns (trimmed headers + explanatory caption). Note value direction differs: high infit underfit, high restscore correlation overfit.

title: easyRasch2 (development version)
text: RMitemInfitCutoff() RMlocdepQ3Cutoff() gain experimental dgp argument — "resample" (default; resample WLE locations simulate, marginal null) vs "conditional" (simulate pattern exact Rasch conditional given observed total score, matched conditional null) — stored result. See dev/q3_dgp_comparison.R / dev/infit_dgp_comparison.R. Q3 plot table outputs now share $matrix / $pairs structure. RMlocdepQ3Plot() returns list two plots: $pairs (per-pair simulated-vs-observed dot-interval, previously single return value) $matrix (lower-triangle diverging-RdYlBu Q3 tile heatmap -cutoff pairs outlined, adapted RASCHplot::ggQ3star(); needs data, else NULL). RMitemICCPlot() reimplemented CML engine, replacing iarm::ICCplot() wrapper. conditional item curve variance now come psychotools CML thresholds + exact conditional distribution given total score. draws confidence intervals observed class-interval means (ci, default ) optional model band (error_band); DIF mode shows per-group CIs annotates panel partial-gamma DIF magnitude (iarm::partgam_DIF, RMdifGamma()), full table attached attr(., "dif_gamma"). New arguments ci, error_band, conf_level, min_n (per-cell floor, 8), items; method / class_intervals / dif_var / output unchanged. conditional-ICC approach follows Buchardt, Christensen & Jensen (2023) RASCHplot package. RMlocdepGamma(): corrected $direction2 caption / @return wording “total - Item2” (note direction 2 lists pairs reverse order); computations unchanged (labelling fix).

Without ggtext, falls back plain element_text(face = "italic") plain “Note.” prefix — markdown asterisks ever leak rendered text. er2_caption() — builds caption string “Note.” prefix wraps long captions 90 characters via strwrap() longer run right edge plot. body text wrapped first prefixed, prefix never broken line break. three helpers internal (@noRd) exported.

Single-item — pass full multi-item dataset use item argument choose item’s curves plot. model still fit items (CML threshold estimation needs full dataset); rendering filtered. category’s label positioned curve’s modal theta (peak bell middle categories; relevant edge monotone extreme categories), clamped small margin plot edges prevent clipping.

title: easyRasch2 0.8.0
text: release renames 22 exported functions consistent domain-prefix → method → variant-suffix scheme autocompletion prefix (RMdif, RMlocdep, RMitem, RMdim, RMplot) surfaces every related function. semantic changes; names. complete mapping new ?easyRasch2-renaming help page. short version: Unchanged: RMreliability(), RMUreliability(), RMtargeting(), RMscoreSE(). Breaking change. deprecation aliases shipped — existing scripts need search--replace. See ?easyRasch2-renaming full table.

title: easyRasch2 0.7.1
text: New RMlocdepQ3plot() visualising per-pair simulated Q3 distributions observed Q3 values, mirroring design RMpgLDplot() RMpgDIFplot(). Supports items n_pairs filters; data supplied, ranks n_pairs |observed Q3 - median(simulated Q3 per pair)|. RMlocdepQ3cutoff() now retains per-pair iteration-level data: returned list gains pair_results, pair_cutoffs, item_names, cutoff_method, hdci_width. Existing scalar outputs (suggested_cutoff etc.) unchanged. New arguments cutoff_method ("hdci" / "quantile") hdci_width (default 0.99) control per-pair credible intervals computed. RMlocdepQ3() can now called full list returned RMlocdepQ3cutoff() (auto-extracts $suggested_cutoff), matching partial-gamma family convention. Numeric scalar still works. RMpartgamLD() RMpgLDplot() gain n_pairs argument keep top-N pairs |gamma| (LD) |observed - median(sim)| deviation (plot). RMpartgamLD(), RMpartgamDIF(), RMitemrestscore() now use consistent column headers: Adj. p-value (BH) new p-value sign. star-string column. RMitemrestscore()’s former Absolute_difference column now signed Difference (observed − expected), - underfit visually distinguishable glance.

New RMciccPlot() conditional item characteristic curves plot, also includes DIF analysis categorical DIF variables. New RMitemHierarchy(), outputs plot illustrating item hierarchy item thresholds confidence intervals. Modified RMcfaCutoff() use .scaled metrics RMSEA CFI, stability. See documentation details. Splits iterations across imputations (e.g. 250 total / 5 imputations = 50 ). title: easyRasch2 0.5.0
text: RMinfitcutoff() gains cutoff_method hdci_width parameters: default (cutoff_method = "hdci", hdci_width = 0.999), per-item cutoff intervals now computed using Highest Density Interval via ggdist::hdci() (99.9% HDCI). Set cutoff_method = "quantile" restore previous behaviour (2.5th/97.5th percentiles). ggdist package required cutoff_method = "hdci" (added Suggests). returned list now also includes cutoff_method hdci_width fields. RMiteminfit() caption updated: cutoff return value RMinfitcutoff(), kable caption now states cutoff method, e.g. "Cutoff values based 250 simulation iterations (99.9% HDCI)." "Cutoff values based 250 simulation iterations (2.5th/97.5th percentile).".

title: easyRasch2 0.4.0
text: RMiteminfit() gains optional cutoff parameter: Accepts return value RMinfitcutoff() ($item_cutoffs data.frame directly). provided, per-item cutoff boundaries (Infit_low, Infit_high) logical Flagged column added "dataframe" "kable" output. kable caption includes number simulation iterations available. New RMinfitcutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination [RMiteminfit()]. Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). Returns per-item 2.5th 97.5th percentiles simulated infit outfit MSQ distributions ($item_cutoffs), together full iteration-level results ($results). Requires iarm package (Suggests).

title: easyRasch2 0.3.0
text: New RMiteminfit(): Computes conditional infit MSQ statistics item via iarm::out_infit(), enriched item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "infit" sorts infit MSQ descending. complete cases used conditional fit calculation. New RMitemrestscore(): Computes observed model-expected item-restscore correlations via iarm::item_restscore(), enriched absolute differences observed expected values, item average locations, item locations relative sample mean person location. Supports dichotomous (Rasch model via eRm::RM()) polytomous (Partial Credit Model via eRm::PCM()) data. Requires iarm package (Suggests). Output options: "kable" (default, plain-text knitr::kable()) "dataframe". Optional sort = "diff" sorts absolute difference descending.

title: easyRasch2 0.2.0
text: RMlocdepQ3(): cutoff now optional (default NULL). omitted, raw Q3 residual correlation matrix returned without dynamic cut-applied. provided, dynamic cut-(mean Q3 + cutoff) shown kable caption . Supersedes requirement always supply cutoff value (PRs #2 #3). New RMlocdepQ3cutoff(): Simulation-based (parametric bootstrap) cutoff determination RMlocdepQ3(). Supports dichotomous polytomous data. Optional parallel processing via mirai (falls back sequential installed). $suggested_cutoff 99th percentile simulated Q3 max–mean distribution. New internal helpers R/utils-simulation.R: sim_poly_item(), sim_partial_score(), extract_item_thresholds(). Dependencies: eRm, psychotools (>= 0.7-3), parallel, utils moved/added Imports. mirai added Suggests.
